The Trump administration on Friday said that it is “80-85 per cent” confident of signing the peace agreement with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war in the Middle East.

news.headline: PTI rejects federal budget for FY2026-27, terms it an 'exercise in elite self-preservation'
news.summary:
<p>ISLAMABAD: PTI, the main opposition party in the National Assembly, on Friday rejected the federal budget for FY2026-27, terming it a “refined exercise in elite self-preservation”.</p>

news.summary: This week, Paris des Arts heads to the Czech Republic to visit Prague, a cultural capital and UNESCO World Heritage city. news.headline: Michigan Army National Guard Uses UAS to Train for Tomorrow's Battlefield
news.summary: Nine soldiers assigned to the 3rd Battalion, 126th Infantry Regiment, participated in a two-week unmanned aerial system program, as the Michigan National Guard expands its drone training initiative.

news.headline: Iran-linked group claims hack of FBI drones, threatens World Cup, monitor reports
news.summary: An Iran-linked hacker group called Handala claimed to have breached FBI drones and threatened to target the 2026 World Cup, monitoring group SITE said Friday.

news.headline: LGBTQ rights in Hungary: 'We are very hopeful that changes will be made'
news.summary: The election of the Hungary's new centre-right prime minister, Peter Magyar, in April marked a particularly stinging defeat for Viktor Orban, whose government was widely regarded as one of the most anti-LGBTQ in the EU. news.headline: 'We need more clean, homegrown, cheap European energy': EU Climate Commissioner
news.summary: With Europeans sizzling in a record-breaking heatwave in May, and lands and rivers facing severe drought conditions in April, the effects of extreme climate-related events are becoming increasingly obvious.
